name: studio
context: Prisma Studio — browser-based GUI for viewing, filtering, editing, and managing database records
info: Prisma Studio is a visual database browser that launches in your web browser, providing a spreadsheet-like interface for viewing, creating, editing, and deleting records across all models in your schema. It connects to your database using the `DATABASE_URL` from your Prisma configuration. Studio supports PostgreSQL, MySQL, and SQLite. It is a development tool — not intended for production use. Run it with `npx prisma studio` and it opens on port 5555 by default. Useful for verifying seed data, debugging queries, and quick manual data entry.
body:
# Prisma Studio

Prisma Studio is a browser-based GUI for exploring and managing your database records.

## Launch

```bash
# Start Studio (opens browser on port 5555)
npx prisma studio

# Custom port
npx prisma studio --port 3333

# Specify browser
npx prisma studio --browser firefox

# Use custom schema path
npx prisma studio --schema ./custom/schema.prisma

# Connect directly to a database URL (no schema needed)
npx prisma studio --url "postgresql://user:pass@localhost:5432/mydb"
```

## Features

- **View all models** — every model from your schema appears as a tab
- **Browse records** — spreadsheet-like grid with pagination
- **Filter records** — filter by field values
- **Sort records** — click column headers to sort
- **Create records** — add new records with a form
- **Edit records** — click cells to edit inline
- **Delete records** — select and delete records
- **View relations** — navigate to related records
- **JSON fields** — view and edit JSON data

## Use Cases

| Scenario | Why Studio |
|----------|------------|
| Verify seed data | Quick visual check that `prisma db seed` worked |
| Debug queries | Check actual database state vs. expected |
| Manual data entry | Add test records without writing code |
| Explore relations | Navigate between related records visually |
| Demo to stakeholders | Show database structure and sample data |

## Supported Databases

- PostgreSQL
- MySQL
- SQLite

## Limitations

- Development tool only — not for production
- No authentication or access control
- No support for MongoDB
- No SQL query editor
- Cannot modify schema (use [[prisma/schema]] and [[prisma/migrate]] for that)

## Environment Variables

| Variable | Purpose |
|----------|--------|
| `DATABASE_URL` | Database to connect to (read from schema) |

sections.title: Launching Studio
sections.body: Run `npx prisma studio` to start. Default port is 5555. Use `--port` for a custom port. Use `--url` to connect directly without a schema file. Studio reads your `prisma/schema.prisma` to discover models and connects using the `DATABASE_URL`.

sections.title: Studio features
sections.body: Studio provides a spreadsheet-like grid for every model. Browse with pagination, filter by field values, sort by columns, create new records with forms, edit inline by clicking cells, delete selected records, and navigate relations. JSON fields render as expandable objects.

links.context: Studio reads the schema to discover models
Use Studio to verify seed data was inserted correctly
Alternative: query programmatically instead of using the GUI
